cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for reporting/export and scope block
preconditions: Testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row applies (FR-27, FR-28, BR-18, BR-19, AC-15, AC-16, AC-17).
Role-matrix fixture provides Lecturer assigned to section A, AcademicAdmin with institution scope, and Student account.
Attendance records exist in assigned section A, unassigned section B, and across institution scope.
steps: As Lecturer, call POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and term-wide filters.
As AcademicAdmin, call POST /v1/exports/attendance with institution-authorized filters.
As Student, call POST /v1/exports/attendance with the same filters.
Complete and inspect each permitted export artifact; inspect denial responses for unauthorized actor.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §3.1 reporting/export and scope row and E2E-08: Lecturer export includes only assigned sections; AcademicAdmin export respects institution scope; Student receives denial without restricted data disclosure (BR-18, BR-19).

cases.title: M07 export service generates scoped CSV rows within real DB transaction
preconditions: Integration database seeded with attendance_records, class_sessions, enrollments, and lecturer-to-section assignments.
M07 Reporting and Export module uses real repository and DB transaction boundaries.
Attendance rows exist in assigned and unassigned sections for the same term.
steps: Invoke M07 createExportJob use-case for Lecturer actor with termId filter and no explicit classSectionId.
Execute export generation pipeline and read persisted export job metadata and generated CSV row set.
Assert repository query predicates restrict classSectionId to lecturer assigned set before row materialization.
Verify zero rows from unassigned sections appear in committed export artifact.
cases.expected: Module integration commits export job and CSV rows scoped to lecturer assigned sections only; CONS-03 scope-aware filters enforced at M07 boundary with no cross-section leakage (BR-18).

cases.title: CSV export schema includes required attendance columns per FR-27
preconditions: Closed session with mixed attendance outcomes (Present, Late, Absent, Manual Present) exists for an assigned section.
M07 export generator configured for MVP csv format.
Integration harness can parse generated CSV header and row values.
steps: Generate CSV export for the section via M07 export pipeline.
Parse CSV header row and validate required columns are present.
Inspect sample rows for student identifier, session reference, attendance status, check-in timestamp, and check-in method fields.
cases.expected: Every exported row includes student identifier, session, status, timestamps, and method as required by FR-27; column names and values are stable and map to attendance_records and class_sessions source data.

cases.title: HTTP contract for POST /v1/exports/attendance returns 202 export job envelope
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Target classSectionId belongs to lecturer assignment set and has attendance data.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId, classSectionId } } and required Idempotency-Key header.
Validate HTTP status code and standard { data, meta, error } response envelope.
Assert data.exportJobId, data.status, and data.format fields per API design §4.6.
Verify meta.requestId and meta.timestamp are present.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 202 with standard envelope; data contains exportJobId, status Queued (or equivalent async state), and format csv; error is null.

cases.title: HTTP contract for Lecturer POST /v1/auth/login then GET /v1/me before export
preconditions: Provisioned Lecturer account with institution-issued credentials exists.
No active bearer token is held by the test client.
steps: Send POST /v1/auth/login with lecturer email and password.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.accessToken, data.expiresInSeconds, and data.roles containing Lecturer.
Send GET /v1/me with Authorization Bearer accessToken from login response.
Assert HTTP 200 with lecturer identity and section scope claims required for export authorization.
cases.expected: Login returns HTTP 200 with accessToken and Lecturer role; GET /v1/me returns HTTP 200 with actor identity and scope claims that gate POST /v1/exports/attendance per FR-27 and BR-18.

cases.title: HTTP contract for roster payload shape aligns with export source data
preconditions: Assigned Lecturer is authenticated.
Open or closed class session with attendance rows exists in lecturer assigned section.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization header.
Validate HTTP 200 and roster envelope with data.classSessionId, data.counts, and data.rows[].
Assert each row includes studentCode, attendanceStatus, checkInMethod, and checkInAt per API §4.5.
Generate POST /v1/exports/attendance for the same section and compare exported CSV values to roster row fields.
cases.expected: Roster response is HTTP 200 with documented payload shape; exported CSV rows for the same session match roster student identifier, status, method, and timestamp values without cross-scope additions.

cases.title: Primary WF-07 workflow: setup enrollments then lecturer CSV export of assigned section
preconditions: AcademicAdmin has created term, course, class section SE101-01, and closed sessions with attendance outcomes.
Lecturer is assigned to SE101-01 only; another section SE101-02 has attendance data outside assignment.
steps: As AcademicAdmin, call POST /v1/enrollments/import for SE101-01 with valid student rows to ensure exportable enrollment baseline.
Authenticate as Lecturer assigned to SE101-01.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and filters { termId, classSectionId: SE101-01 }.
Wait for export completion, download CSV, and verify every row belongs to SE101-01 with required columns.
cases.expected: End-to-end WF-07 happy path succeeds: enrollment import establishes eligible students; lecturer CSV export contains only SE101-01 attendance rows with student identifier, session, status, timestamps, and method (testing-plan E2E-08).

cases.title: Alternate path: AcademicAdmin institution-scoped export with status filter
preconditions: AcademicAdmin is authenticated with authorized institution scope spanning multiple faculties.
Attendance data exists across multiple sections including Absent and Present statuses.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and filters { termId, status: Absent } as AcademicAdmin.
Complete export job and parse CSV output.
Verify all rows have status Absent and classSectionId values within institution authorized scope.
Confirm no rows from sections outside institution grant appear.
cases.expected: AcademicAdmin export succeeds with institution-scoped Absent-only rows; scope filters are applied before query execution and output excludes unauthorized sections (BR-18 AcademicAdmin branch).

cases.title: Successful CSV export writes audit log entry with actor, scope, and format
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to a section with exportable attendance data.
Audit log query access is available for verification actor.
steps: Record audit log count for ExportCreated or equivalent action type before export.
Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and scoped filters; wait for job completion.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by actorUserId, actionType, and exportJobId correlation.
Inspect audit entry for actor, timestamp, scope summary, and format csv.
cases.expected: Every successful export produces immutable AuditLog entry with actor, timestamp, scope, and format per BR-18 and AC-17; audit row correlates to exportJobId without exposing foreign-scope data.

cases.title: Student denied institution-wide CSV export
preconditions: Student user is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Institution-wide attendance export data exists across multiple sections.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: csv, filters: { termId } } as Student.
Validate HTTP status code and error envelope.
Query export job persistence for any job created by the denied request.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with error.code Forbidden or OutOfScope; data is null with no exportJobId; no export job row or CSV artifact is created (BR-18 Student denied, BR-19).

cases.title: Lecturer denied export for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B has attendance data and is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId, format: csv } as lecturer.
Inspect HTTP status, error.code, and response body for restricted data leakage.
Verify no export job or partial CSV artifact is produced.
cases.expected: Request returns HTTP 403 OutOfScope or Forbidden; response contains no section B student identifiers, attendance statuses, or exportJobId (PRM-10, BR-19).

cases.title: Unsupported export format rejected with UnsupportedFormat error
preconditions: Lecturer or AcademicAdmin is authenticated with valid export permission.
MVP supports csv format only per VR-RP-03.
steps: Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with body { format: xlsx, filters: { termId } }.
Validate HTTP status and error envelope.
Confirm no export job is queued.
cases.expected: Response is HTTP 400 or 422 with error.code UnsupportedFormat; error message or details list csv as allowed format; no exportJobId is returned.

cases.title: Out-of-scope filter rejected before export query executes
preconditions: DepartmentAdmin scoped to faculty X is authenticated.
Attendance data exists in faculty Y section outside department scope.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters targeting faculty Y classSectionId.
Parse 403 response and any error.details field.
Verify export job table has no new row and no CSV rows were materialized.
cases.expected: Scope validation short-circuits with HTTP 403 OutOfScope before unrestricted query; error.details contain no faculty Y attendance samples or row counts (VR-RP-02, BR-19).

cases.title: Duplicate export request with same Idempotency-Key returns existing job
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated with export permission for a section.
Idempotency-Key header support is enabled on POST /v1/exports/attendance per API-G03.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with a fixed Idempotency-Key and scoped csv filters.
Immediately submit an identical request with the same Idempotency-Key in parallel.
Compare exportJobId values and count persisted export job rows for the key.
cases.expected: Both responses return the same exportJobId; only one export job row is persisted; no duplicate CSV artifacts are generated for the same idempotency key.

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey triggers scoped CSV export with ExportScopeSummary
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Reporting/export UI (SUR-05) shows term filter and section selector populated from lecturer scope.
Attendance data exists in assigned and unassigned sections.
steps: Navigate to attendance report or export page.
Select an assigned section and confirm ExportScopeSummary displays section and row scope before export.
Choose CSV format and trigger export action.
Confirm job status or download UI and verify exported file contains only assigned-section rows with required columns.
cases.expected: UI section selector lists only assigned class sections; ExportScopeSummary confirms scope before execution; CSV download succeeds with lecturer-scoped attendance data and no unassigned section rows.

cases.title: Student browser journey blocked from CSV export action
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile or desktop browser.
Application shell may expose or deep-link to reporting surfaces.
Student role lacks ExportJob.execute per permissions matrix.
steps: Attempt to navigate directly to attendance export URL.
Inspect navigation menu for export affordances.
If export UI is reachable, attempt to trigger CSV export.
Observe user-facing denial message and network response.
cases.expected: Student cannot execute institution CSV export; export action is hidden, disabled, or fails with access-denied feedback without displaying other students' attendance data or partial export download.
